title: Bounded Disjoint-Scope Simultaneous-Worker Planning
objective: Define the exact ownership, non-overlap, validation, conflict, coordinator, and publication rules needed to decide whether a later simultaneous multi-worker pilot packet can be authored.
planning_summary: A later simultaneous-worker execution packet is conditionally authorable after this planning authority is published, but only if it preserves exact apparatus/relay ownership, keeps static surfaces coordinator-owned, runs coordinator merge validation, and aborts on any shared-file or package/toolchain need.
non_overlap_rules: No worker may edit a file listed in another worker's owned source or validation set.
No worker may edit coordinator-owned static-surfaces validation, routing, roadmap, packet, handoff, package, lockfile, app layout, global style, Playwright config, or Next config files.

Any need to touch a shared helper, fixture, API route, app layout, global style, package, lockfile, or static-surfaces validation file aborts the simultaneous-worker pilot and requires a new planning packet.
Both workers must produce independently reviewable diffs whose changed-file sets remain disjoint before coordinator integration.
Coordinator publication and host reconciliation remain single-owner and happen only after all worker artifacts are validated and a later packet explicitly selects publication.
validation_sequence: Each worker runs `git diff --check` over only its owned slice before handoff.
Each worker may run only existing no-install validation commands.
Coordinator integration must run `git diff --check` over both worker slices plus coordinator-owned authority files.
Coordinator integration must run `apps/operations-web/node_modules/.bin/tsc.cmd --noEmit` on the workstation when no-install tools are present.
Coordinator integration must run `apps/operations-web/node_modules/.bin/next.cmd build` on the workstation when no-install tools are present.

Coordinator integration must run focused Playwright over `tests/browser-shell.apparatus.smoke.spec.ts`, `tests/browser-shell.relay.smoke.spec.ts`, and `tests/browser-shell.static-surfaces.smoke.spec.ts` before any publication decision.
If any executable validation is unavailable under no-install rules, the pilot must stop at validated-scope or blocker-recorded state and may not publish on diff hygiene alone.
conflict_and_abort_criteria: Abort if both workers need the same file.
Abort if either worker needs package, lockfile, install, package-manager activation/download, runtime, service, migration, or old-clone mutation.
Abort if either worker needs `apps/operations-web/tests/browser-shell.static-surfaces.smoke.spec.ts` or another shared validation file.
Abort if either worker needs app-wide layout, CSS, test config, Next config, or shared fixtures not explicitly owned in this packet.
Abort if coordinator cannot prove changed-file disjointness before integration.
Abort if workstation mirror validation cannot run or cannot prove the exact integrated artifact before any publication decision.
residual_overlap: The workers still share the same Next application runtime during validation.
The focused Playwright run still uses one web server process.
Static-surfaces validation remains a cross-lane smoke surface and is therefore coordinator-owned.
App root, global styles, package files, lockfiles, Playwright config, and Next config remain shared and outside worker ownership.
next_packet: Olares Phase 5 083 - Packet 080 Through Packet 082 Authority Publication And Host Mirror Resync Gate
preserved_boundaries: No simultaneous multi-worker source/test execution opens in Packet 082.
No second mutation worker execution opens in Packet 082.
No source/test execution opens in Packet 082.
No package or lockfile mutation opens.
No installs or package-manager activation/download open.
No rollback, force, reset, or clean opens.
sql_permission_note: This planning packet authorizes no database writes and no source/test execution, simultaneous multi-worker mutation, second-worker execution, migration approval, runtime mutation, service mutation, package mutation, lockfile mutation, install, package-manager activation/download, remote rewrite, AI-services expansion, Gitea/code-hosting transition, canonical-hosting transition, rollback, force/reset/clean, or old-clone mutation.
